sections.title: Talking to Juna while collecting tears

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: I will not permit you to be in the cave for long. Collect as many tears as you can.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I want to get out now.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Tell me... a story...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay...

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Perhaps you should empty your hands before you begin.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Your story has entertained me. But it is a poor sort of adventurer who only tells stories of the past and does not find new stories to tell. I will not let you into the cave again until you have had more adventures!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: It has not been long since your last visit. Come back [tomorrow/in [amount] days].

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: I fear you will have no new stories to tell me. Come again when you have had more adventures.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: I fear you will have no new stories to tell me. Come again [tomorrow/in [amount] days] if you have had more adventures by then.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Your stories have entertained me. I will let you into the cave for a short time.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: I must warn you though, the Tears cannot help you with your sailing abilities at this time, for Guthix does not wish to interfere with the balance.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I understand.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Collect as much as you can from the blue streams. If you let in water from the green streams, it will take away from the blue. For Guthix is god of balance, and balance lies in the juxtaposition of opposites.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll come back another day.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: As you wish, [player name].

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Collect as much as you can from the blue streams. If you let in water from the green streams, it will take away from the blue. For Guthix is god of balance, and balance lies in the juxtaposition of opposites.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You tell me a story.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: The Third Age of the world was a time of great conflict, of destruction never seen before or since, when all the gods save Guthix warred for control.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: The colossal Wyrms, of whom today's dragons are a pale reflection, turned all the sky to fire, while on the ground armies of foot soldiers, goblins and trolls and humans, filled the valleys and plains with blood.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: In time the noise of the conflict woke Guthix from His deep slumber, and He rose and stood in the centre of the battlefield so that the splendour of His wrath filled the world, and He called for the conflict to cease!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Silence fell, for the gods knew that none could challenge the power of the mighty Guthix -- for His power is that of nature itself, to which all other things are subject, in the end.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Guthix reclaimed that which had been stolen from Him, and went back underground to return to His sleep and continue to draw the world's power into Himself.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: But on His way into the depths of the earth He sat and rested in this cave; and, thinking of the battle-scarred desert that now stretched from one side of His world to the other, He wept.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: And so great was His sorrow, and so great was His life- giving power, that the rocks themselves began to weep with Him.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Later, Guthix noticed that the rocks continued to weep, and that their tears were infused with a small part of His power.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: So He set me, His servant, to guard the cave, and He entrusted to me the task of judging who was and was not worthy to access the tears.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the Tears of Guthix.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: I will tell you the story of the light-creatures.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Myriad and beautiful were the creatures and civilizations of the early ages of the world. Gielinor was a work of art, shaped lovingly over the millennia by the creative mind of Guthix.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Only the sturdiest races survived the Godwars, and even then only by abandoning their high culture and gearing their societies towards war. Of the more delicate races there is now no trace, and almost no memory.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: One such race had bodies as fragile as snowflakes, yet they built crystal cities that stood for a thousand years.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: The wind would whisper through the spires and fill them with sweet harmonies, and the rising sun would shine through the precious gems that studded the towers and create inter plays of light as if rainbows were dancing.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Indeed, so marvellous was this light-show at its height that the patterns of light themselves became alive, and great flocks of luminous creatures rode along the gem- cast beams, each drawn to its own colour.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: The creatures you see floating in this chasm are the last sorry remnants of that age. I do not know how they made their way here and survived to this time, but I am grateful for their company.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the light-creatures.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Not long after the start of my vigil a party of goblins happened on my cave. These were the ugly brutes of the gods' armies, but their armour bore a faded patch where the symbol of a god had been removed.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: They looked around hesitantly, squinting in the light of crude torches, but as soon as they saw me they raised their spears and charged.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: No one may access the Tears of Guthix by force. I sent them tumbling into the chasm.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Two hundred years later another goblin found me. This one was unarmed, and although she was very wary I succeeded in engaging her in conversation.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Since then I have had many more visits from the descendants of those lost warriors, and over the centuries I have seen them change.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: They have become timid rather than aggressive, and I have seen the light of intelligence grow in their bulging eyes.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Their stories are repetitive and grim, of scraping a living out of the harsh rock and through ingenuity and toil shaping it into a home.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: I have followed the progress of their race, but their individual stories hold little interest for me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the Cave Goblins.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to receive messages prompting me to return here.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Very well, when you are eligible to drink from the Tears, you shall be reminded daily.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't want any messages reminding me to return here.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Very well, it will be up to you to remember when you can return. It is good that you are willing to take responsibility for yourself in this way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you do something with this icon?

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: So many aeons have passed since these were made. So many mortals have been born, lived their lives, then died as Balance requires. What stories they must have known!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: I would be happy to honour the dead by combining such an icon with your Staff of the Dead. Give the items to me, if you wish.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, here you go...

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: I would happily honour the dead, and all the stories they must have known, by combining your Staff of the Dead with your icon of my master, Guthix. But this cannot be undone; they will be bound forever.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Combine them. I understand it can't be undone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe another time.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: I would be happy to honour the dead by combining such an icon with a Staff of the Dead, if you bring one to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: My weapon is sort of like a Staff of the Dead, don't you think?

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: It was, but I appear to have combined it with an icon already.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: I cannot combine it with that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got a weapon in my inventory that's sort of like a Staff of the Dead.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: It is not what I seek.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A Staff of the Dead, you say? Okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not now.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: If you wish to tell me more stories, come out here first.

sections.title: Staff of the dead without having a Guthixian icon

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: A Staff of the Dead! I would happily honour the dead, and all the stories they must have known, by combining your staff with an icon of my master, Guthix.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Perhaps you can find such an icon and bring it to me.

sections.title: Guthixian icon without having a staff of the dead

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you do something with this icon?

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: So many aeons have passed since these were made. So many mortals have been born, lived their lives, then died as Balance requires. What stories they must have known!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: I would happily honour the dead by combining such an icon with a Staff of the Dead, if you bring one to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A Staff of the Dead, you say? Okay, thanks.

sections.title: Guthixian icon or a staff of the dead while having both

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: I would happily honour the dead, and all the stories they must have known, by combining your Staff of the Dead with your icon of my master, Guthix. But this cannot be undone; they will be bound forever.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Combine them. I understand it can't be undone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

sections.title: Guthixian icon while having any other bladed staff equipped

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you do something with this icon?

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: So many aeons have passed since these were made. So many mortals have been born, lived their lives, then died as Balance requires. What stories they must have known!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: I would happily honour the dead by combining such an icon with a Staff of the Dead, if you bring one to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: My weapon is sort of like a Staff of the Dead, don't you think?

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: It was, but I appear to have combined it with an icon already.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: I cannot combine it with that.

sections.title: Guthixian icon while having any other bladed staff in the inventory

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you do something with this icon?

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: So many aeons have passed since these were made. So many mortals have been born, lived their lives, then died as Balance requires. What stories they must have known!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: I would be happy to honour the dead by combining such an icon with a Staff of the Dead, if you bring one to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've got a weapon in my inventory that's sort of like a Staff of the Dead.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: It is not what I seek.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: I can do nothing more with that.

sections.title: Staff of light, Toxic staff (uncharged) or Toxic staff of the dead with a Guthixian icon in the inventory

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: I cannot combine your icon with that weapon.

sections.title: Using a Staff of light, Toxic staff (uncharged) or Toxic staff of the dead without Guthixian icon, or using any other item on Juna

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: I do not need that from you.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Hey, I have something for you but you don't have any space for it!

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Your dedication to balance is astounding adventurer.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Here you go.

lines.speaker: Juna
lines.text: Your dedication to balance is astounding adventurer.
